pkgstats 0.0.0.147

    • Installing ctags and GNU global

Installing ctags and GNU global

Mark Padgham

2021-06-08

Source: vignettes/installation.Rmd
installation.Rmd

The pkgstats package requires installation of the two system libraries, universal-ctags and GNU global. These libraries are very small and generally relatively quick to install, even when compiled from source. Installation is nevertheless not straightforward because universal-ctags is a fork of another project, and is not officially supported on all systems. This vignette describes how to install these necessary libraries on Linux, MacOS, and Windows operating systems.

Linux

The easiest way to install these libraries on any Linux system is to compile them both from source, for which the procedure is identical for all Linux operating systems. First compile and install ctags with the following lines:

git clone https://github.com/universal-ctags/ctags.git
cd ctags
./autogen.sh
./configure --prefix=/usr
make
sudo make install

GNU global can generally be installed from most Linux package managers, for example through apt-get install global for Ubuntu, or pacman -S global for Archlinux.

MaxOS

brew install --HEAD universal-ctags/universal-ctags/universal-ctags
brew install global

Windows via chocalatey

The easiest way to install the required libraries on Windows is via chocalatey:

choco install universal-ctags global

Windows via msys2

Installation on Windows without using chocalatey requires msys2 which is installated as part of Rtools40. The following lines will download, compile, and install universal-ctags.

git clone https://github.com/universal-ctags/ctags.git
cd ctags
./autogen.sh
./configure --prefix=/usr
make V=1
make install

GNU global can then be downloaded as a pre-compiled binary from http://adoxa.altervista.org/global/ (generally as the latest Win32 version - there is no 64-bit version). The downloaded file then just needs to be unzipped and installed.

Contents

Developed by Mark Padgham.
